(Portuguese pronunciation: [[ˈɡɾu.pu ˈɡlo.bu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); lit. 'Globe Group'), formerly and still legally known as **Organizações Globo**[[3]](#cite_note-3) ([[oʁ.ɡa.ni.zaˈsõj̃s ˈɡlo.bu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); lit. 'Globe Organization'[[4]](#cite_note-4)), is a Brazilian [private](/wiki/Privately_held_company "Privately held company") [entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment") and [mass media](/wiki/Mass_media "Mass media") [conglomerate](/wiki/Conglomerate_(company) "Conglomerate (company)") based in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. Founded in 1925 by Irineu Marinho, it is the largest media group in Latin America, and one of the world's largest [media conglomerates](/wiki/Media_conglomerate "Media conglomerate").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Brazilian media group

This article is about Brazilian media conglomerate. For the subsidiary that own television networks and digital services, see [Globo (media company)](/wiki/Globo_(media_company) "Globo (media company)").

Not to be confused with [Global Media Group](/wiki/Global_Media_Group "Global Media Group") in Portugal.

**Grupo Globo** (Portuguese pronunciation: [[ˈɡɾu.pu ˈɡlo.bu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); lit. 'Globe Group'), formerly and still legally known as **Organizações Globo**[[3]](#cite_note-3) ([[oʁ.ɡa.ni.zaˈsõj̃s ˈɡlo.bu]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Portuguese "Help:IPA/Portuguese"); lit. 'Globe Organization'[[4]](#cite_note-4)), is a Brazilian [private](/wiki/Privately_held_company "Privately held company") [entertainment](/wiki/Entertainment "Entertainment") and [mass media](/wiki/Mass_media "Mass media") [conglomerate](/wiki/Conglomerate_(company) "Conglomerate (company)") based in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), Brazil. Founded in 1925 by Irineu Marinho, it is the largest media group in Latin America, and one of the world's largest [media conglomerates](/wiki/Media_conglomerate "Media conglomerate").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Grupo Globo's assets comprise over-the-air broadcasting, television and film production, pay television subscription service, streaming media, publishing, and online services. Its main properties include media company [Globo](/wiki/Globo_(media_company) "Globo (media company)"), consiting of the flagship television network [TV Globo](/wiki/TV_Globo "TV Globo"); the streaming service [Globoplay](/wiki/Globoplay "Globoplay"), pay television content unit [Canais Globo](/wiki/Canais_Globo "Canais Globo"), consisting of cable television networks such as [GloboNews](/wiki/GloboNews "GloboNews"), [GNT](/wiki/GNT "GNT"), [Multishow](/wiki/Multishow "Multishow"), [SporTV](/wiki/SporTV "SporTV"), [Viva](/wiki/Viva_(Brazilian_TV_channel) "Viva (Brazilian TV channel)"), [Gloob](/wiki/Gloob "Gloob"), and the premium film network [Telecine](/wiki/Rede_Telecine "Rede Telecine"); film production company [Globo Filmes](/wiki/Globo_Filmes "Globo Filmes"); radio operator [Sistema Globo de Rádio](/wiki/Sistema_Globo_de_R%C3%A1dio "Sistema Globo de Rádio") and magazine and newspaper publisher, [Editora Globo](/wiki/Editora_Globo "Editora Globo"), including [Infoglobo](/w/index.php?title=Infoglobo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Infoglobo (page does not exist)") [[pt](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Infoglobo "pt:Infoglobo")].

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grupo_Globo&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/3e/Globo100_English.svg/250px-Globo100_English.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Globo100_English.svg)

Logo of Globo's 100th anniversary (2025)

The company's first enterprise was the newspaper *[A Noite](/wiki/A_Noite "A Noite")*. With its success, publishing in the late afternoon, Irineu Marinho decided to launch the morning daily *[O Globo](/wiki/O_Globo "O Globo")* in 1925. After his sudden death, just weeks after its launch, his son, [Roberto Pisani Marinho](/wiki/Roberto_Marinho "Roberto Marinho"), became the company's director. Working actively in the media business, Roberto Marinho decided to invest in other areas and launched Radio Globo in 1944. However, the company only became recognizable nationwide after the launching of Rede Globo (now known as [TV Globo](/wiki/TV_Globo "TV Globo")), the world's second-largest commercial TV network in 1965.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The company is currently run by the sons of Roberto Marinho: [Roberto Irineu Marinho](/wiki/Roberto_Irineu_Marinho "Roberto Irineu Marinho"), [João Roberto Marinho](/wiki/Jo%C3%A3o_Roberto_Marinho "João Roberto Marinho") and [José Roberto Marinho](/wiki/Jos%C3%A9_Roberto_Marinho "José Roberto Marinho"). In May 2013, a study released by media agency [ZenithOptimedia](/wiki/ZenithOptimedia "ZenithOptimedia") showed Globo occupied the 17th place in a list of the top global media owners. It was the first time the company appeared on this ranking.[[10]](#cite_note-10) This ranking remained in 2015,[[11]](#cite_note-11) but in 2017 the company's fell to 19th place,[[12]](#cite_note-12) and in June of the same year Grupo Globo signed a joint venture deal with [Vice Media](/wiki/Vice_Media "Vice Media").[[13]](#cite_note-13)

## Assets owned by Grupo Globo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grupo_Globo&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Assets owned by Grupo Globo")]

* [Bis](/wiki/Bis_(TV_channel) "Bis (TV channel)") [[pt](https://pt.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bis_(canal_de_televis%C3%A3o) "pt:Bis (canal de televisão)")]
* [Canal Brasil](/wiki/Canal_Brasil "Canal Brasil")

  + [Universal TV](/wiki/Universal_TV "Universal TV")
  + [Studio Universal](/wiki/Studio_Universal_(Latin_America) "Studio Universal (Latin America)")
  + [USA](/wiki/USA_Network_(Latin_American_TV_channel) "USA Network (Latin American TV channel)")

  + [Globo Portugal](/wiki/Globo_(Portuguese_TV_channel) "Globo (Portuguese TV channel)")
    - [Globo Now](/wiki/Globo_Now "Globo Now")
  + [Globo On](/wiki/Globo_On "Globo On") (Africa)

#### Film and television production

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grupo_Globo&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Film and television production")]

* [Estúdios Globo](/wiki/Est%C3%BAdios_Globo "Estúdios Globo")
* [Globo Filmes](/wiki/Globo_Filmes "Globo Filmes")

### Former properties

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Grupo_Globo&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Former properties")]

* [Som Livre](/wiki/Som_Livre "Som Livre") (Acquired by [Sony Music](/wiki/Sony_Music "Sony Music") in April 2021)[[16]](#cite_note-16)
* [SKY Brasil](/wiki/SKY_Brasil "SKY Brasil") ([Vrio Corp.](/wiki/Vrio_Corp. "Vrio Corp.") acquired the 7% stake in Grupo Globo)
* [Telemontecarlo](/wiki/La7 "La7") (1985–1994, acquired by [Vittorio Cecchi Gori](/wiki/Vittorio_Cecchi_Gori "Vittorio Cecchi Gori"))
* [Retequattro](/wiki/Retequattro "Retequattro") (1982-1985?), acquired by [Silvio Berlusconi Communications](/w/index.php?title=Silvio_Berlusconi_Communications&action=edit&redlink=1 "Silvio Berlusconi Communications (page does not exist)"), now [Mediaset](/wiki/Mediaset "Mediaset")
